sourceMovie.title: 5 Centimeters per Second
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto 5 Centimeters per Second (2007) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ocean Waves
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 5 Centimeters per Second clicked for you, Ocean Waves (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ride Your Wave
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 5 Centimeters per Second clicked for you, Ride Your Wave (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 5 Centimeters per Second?
faq.answer: Try Ocean Waves, Ride Your Wave, The Virgin Suicides—each shares DNA with 5 Centimeters per Second while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 5 Centimeters per Second?
faq.answer: 5 Centimeters per Second (2007) is best described as Animation, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 5 Centimeters per Second worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
